data.name: AllowItemGroupRepeatKeyReuse
data.value: Allow ItemGroup Repeat Keys reuse?

data.name: AllowItemGroupRepeatKeyReuseDescription
data.value: Yes – will reuse the first available ItemGroup Repeat Key even if it was inserted and removed before, No – will continue from the last existing ItemGroup Repeat Key at the moment

data.name: AllowStudyEventRepeatKeyReuse
data.value: Allow StudyEvent Repeat Keys reuse?

data.name: AllowStudyEventRepeatKeyReuseDescription
data.value: Yes – will reuse the first available StudyEvent Repeat Key even if it was inserted and removed before, No – will continue from the last existing StudyEvent Repeat Key at the moment

data.name: DataTransferFormRepeatKeyCategory
data.value: Data Transfer Generic Form Repeat Key

data.name: DataTransferItemGroupRepeatKeyCategory
data.value: Data Transfer Generic ItemGroup Repeat Key

data.name: DataTransferStudyEventRepeatKeyCategory
data.value: Data Transfer Generic StudyEvent Repeat Key

data.name: ExistingItemGroupRepeatKeys
data.value: Existing ItemGroup Repeat Keys

data.name: ExistingItemGroupRepeatKeysDescription
data.value: The list of the ItemGroup Repeat Keys that already exist in the client’s system, and should be updated, rather than inserted (comma separated list of existing RepeatKeys)

data.name: FirstItemGroupRepeatKeyFormat
data.value: First Generic ItemGroup Repeat Key Format

data.name: FirstItemGroupRepeatKeyFormatDescription
data.value: Will be applied to the first ItemGroup Repeat Key in the folder (they are different in some studies). Can be empty, which means there is no different First ItemGroup Repeat Key format (ex. "RK{0}"). Note: will be overwritten by the same Visit setting

data.name: FirstStudyEventRepeatKeyFormat
data.value: First Generic StudyEvent Repeat Key Format

data.name: FirstStudyEventRepeatKeyFormatDescription
data.value: Will be applied to the first StudyEvent Repeat Key in the folder (they are different in some studies). Can be empty, which means there is no different First StudyEvent Repeat Key format. {0} - RepeatKey, {1} - Scheduled RepeatKey, {2} - Unscheduled RepeatKey, {3} – ReplacementRepeatKey, {4} – ScreenFailRepeatKey, {5} – InformedConsentRepeatKey (ex. "P{0}[1]/DAYN6N1P{0}[1]"). Note: will be overwritten by the same Visit setting

data.name: FormRepeatKeyFormat
data.value: Generic Form Repeat Key Format

data.name: FormRepeatKeyFormatDescription
data.value: Free text constant string. Note: will be overwritten by the same Visit setting

data.name: FormRepeatKeysEnabled
data.value: Generic Form Repeat Keys Enabled

data.name: FormRepeatKeysEnabledDescription
data.value: Indicates if the Form RepeatKey attributes should be generated and included in the output

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyBasis
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Basis

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyBasisDescription
data.value: Study - will be started from the initial value once in the Study, Site - will be started from the initial value for every Site, Subject - will be started from the initial value for every Subject, SubjectVisit - will be started from the initial value for every SubjectVisit

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCounterBasis
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Counter Basis

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCounterBasisDescription
data.value: Drug Unit Id – assigned drugs will be counted, SubjectVisit – every SubjectVisit trigger performed will be counted (helpful for Informed Consent Repeat Key, Unblinding Repeat Key, Non-serialized Drug Repeat Key, constant Repeat Key), Screen Fail Reason – every SF reason will be counted (helpful for SF Reason Repeat Key), Dose – every captured Dose will be counted

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCounterInitialValue
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Counter Initial Value

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCounterInitialValueDescription
data.value: The number that is used as the ItemGroup Repeat Key Counter start value

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCountSkippedVisits
data.value: Count Skipped Visits for ItemGroup Repeat Key

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyCountSkippedVisitsDescription
data.value: Indicates if SubjectVisits with VisitDate = null should be processed by ItemGroup Repeat Key counter

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyFormat
data.value: Generic ItemGroup Repeat Key Format

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyFormatDescription
data.value: Generic ItemGroup Repeat Key Format (ex. "RK{0}"). Note: will be overwritten by the same Visit setting

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyInsertTransactionType
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Insert TransactionType

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyInsertTransactionTypeDescription
data.value: Used to add new Repeat Key information in the client’s system

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyOverwriteManuallyReplacedDrug
data.value: Overwrite the manually replaced Drug Unit for the ItemGroup Repeat Key?

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyOverwriteManuallyReplacedDrugDescription
data.value: Yes - will take the ItemGroup Repeat Key of the Replaced Drug Unit (will overwrite the drug info in the client’s system, TransactionType will be forced to be set to Update). No – will increment the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the replaced drug to the first available ItemGroup Repeat Key (inserts a separate drug info row in the client’s system)

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyOverwriteReplacedDrug
data.value: Overwrite the replaced Drug Unit for the ItemGroup Repeat Key?

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyOverwriteReplacedDrugDescription
data.value: Yes – will use the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the replaced drug (will overwrite the drug info in the client’s system), TransactionType will be forced to be set to Update. No – will increment the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the replaced drug to the first available ItemGroup Repeat Key (inserts a separate drug info row in the client’s system). Note: applicable only if Replacement Visits Strategy is Replaced Drug Unit Id

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyRemoveTransactionType
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Remove TransactionType

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyRemoveTransactionTypeDescription
data.value: Used for backouts/manual unassignment/manual replacement removals to remove the Repeat Key information from the client's system

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyReplacementVisitsStrategy
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Replacement Visits Strategy

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyReplacementVisitsStrategyDescription
data.value: Independent – increments the ItemGroup Repeat Key value for the Replacement visits independently (V1 – RK1, V2 – RK1, RK2, RV1 – RK1), Parent Subject Visit Id – increments the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the first DrugUnit from the Parent Subject Visit (V1 – RK1, V2 – RK1, RK2, RV1 – RK3), Replaced Drug Unit Id (Has no affect on the self-support actions) – takes (increments/replaces) the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the replaced DrugUnitId (V1 – RK1, V2 – RK1, RK2, RV1 – RK{ReplacedDrugUnit_RK})

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyResendManuallyReplacedDrugOnDelete
data.value: Resend the replaced Drug information on the Manual Drug Replacement removal?

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyResendManuallyReplacedDrugOnDeleteDescription
data.value: Yes – will resend the information of the overwritten drug with the same Repeat Key, TransactionType will be forced to be set to Update. No – will send empty information, TransactionType will be forced to be set to Remove

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyResendReplacedDrugOnBackout
data.value: Resend the replaced Drug Information on the Replacement visit backout?

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyResendReplacedDrugOnBackoutDescription
data.value: Yes – will resend the information of the overwritten Drug with the same ItemGroup Repeat Key, TransactionType will be forced to be set to Update. No – will send empty information as for the other visit backouts, TransactionType will be forced to be set to Remove. Note: applicable only if Replacement Visits Strategy is Replaced Drug Unit Id

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysDrugCodesToExclude
data.value: Drug Codes To Exclude from ItemGroup Repeat Keys

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysDrugCodesToExcludeDescription
data.value: The list of the drug codes that should not be processed by ItemGroup Repeat Keys counter

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysEnabled
data.value: Generic ItemGroup Repeat Keys Enabled

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysEnabledDescription
data.value: Indicates if the ItemGroup RepeatKey attributes should be generated and included in the output

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysVisitsToExclude
data.value: Visits To Exclude from the ItemGroup Repeat Keys

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysVisitsToExcludeDescription
data.value: The list of the visit ids that should be excluded for the ItemGroup Repeat Keys counter

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysVisitsToInclude
data.value: Visits To Include to the ItemGroup Repeat Keys

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeysVisitsToIncludeDescription
data.value: The list of the visit ids that should be included for the ItemGroup Repeat Keys counter (all visits are included if empty)

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyUnscheduledVisitsStrategy
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Unscheduled Visits Strategy

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyUnscheduledVisitsStrategyDescription
data.value: Independent – increments the ItemGroup Repeat Key value for the Unscheduled visits independently (V1 – RK1, V2 – RK1, RK2, UV1 – RK1), Parent Subject Visit Id – Unscheduled visits will be incrementing the ItemGroup Repeat Key value of the Parent Subject Visit (V1 – RK1, V2 – RK1, RK2, UV1 – RK3)

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyUpdateTransactionType
data.value: ItemGroup Repeat Key Update TransactionType

data.name: ItemGroupRepeatKeyUpdateTransactionTypeDescription
data.value: Used to overwrite the existing Repeat Key information in the client’s system

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyBasis
data.value: StudyEvent Repeat Key Basis

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyBasisDescription
data.value: Study - will be started from the initial value once in the Study, Site - will be started from the initial value for every Site, Subject - will be started from the initial value for every Subject, SubjectVisit - will be started from the initial value for every SubjectVisit

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyCounterInitialValue
data.value: StudyEvent Repeat Key Counter Initial Value

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyCounterInitialValueDescription
data.value: The number that is used as the StudyEvent Repeat Key Counter start value

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyFormat
data.value: Generic StudyEvent Repeat Key Format

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeyFormatDescription
data.value: {0} - RepeatKey, {1} - Scheduled RepeatKey, {2} - Unscheduled RepeatKey, {3} – ReplacementRepeatKey, {4} – ScreenFailRepeatKey, {5} – InformedConsentRepeatKey (ex. "P{0}[1]/DAYN6N1[1]"). Note: will be overwritten by the same Visit setting

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeysEnabled
data.value: Generic StudyEvent Repeat Keys Enabled

data.name: StudyEventRepeatKeysEnabledDescription
data.value: Indicates if the StudyEvent RepeatKey attributes should be generated and included in the output
